problem.title: Multiply Strings
problem.description: Given two non-negative integers num1 and num2 represented as strings, return the product of num1 and num2, also represented as a string. You must not use any built-in BigInteger library or convert the inputs to integer directly.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Grade School Multiplication
solutions.description: Simulate long multiplication digit by digit: the product of digits at positions i and j (from the right) contributes to result positions i+j and i+j+1. Accumulate into an integer array of size m+n, handle carries in-place, then convert to a string skipping leading zeros. The key insight is the index mapping — multiplying the i-th digit from the end of num1 by the j-th digit from the end of num2 always lands at position i+j+1 with carry at i+j.
solutions.code_python:
def multiply(num1: str, num2: str) -> str:
    m, n = len(num1), len(num2)
    result = [0] * (m + n)
    for i in range(m - 1, -1, -1):
        for j in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
            product = (ord(num1[i]) - ord('0')) * (ord(num2[j]) - ord('0'))
            total = result[i + j + 1] + product
            result[i + j + 1] = total % 10
            result[i + j] += total // 10
    # Skip leading zeros
    digits = ''.join(str(d) for d in result).lstrip('0')
    return digits if digits else '0'
